    Vadim Biryukov

    PhD Candidate in Computer Science at Illinois Institute of Technology. Research focus: system memory architecture, memory hierarchy optimization, and hardware prefetchers.

    • Chicago, IL
    • Illinois Institute of Technology
    • Email
    • GitHub
    • LinkedIn

    Projects

    Core expertise in systems programming and computer architecture; additional experience across machine learning, blockchain, and web domains.

    ProgrammingC++, Python, Java, MASM, Go, COBOL
    Research ToolsChampSim, Intel Pin Tool, GDB, Perf, Valgrind, Overleaf
    Systems & ArchitectureLinux, WSL, VPS, NAS, VMware, VirtualBox, Vagrant, Docker
    Compilers & BuildClang, GCC, Make, Bash
    ML & FrameworksTensorFlow, PyTorch, vLLM
    Web & ApplicationHTML5, CSS, JavaScript, Django, React, Laravel, PHP
    BlockchainSolidity, Web3.js, OpenZeppelin, MetaMask, Moralis
    Cloud & DatabasesAzure, Chameleon Cloud, Digital Ocean, MySQL, SQL Server, Firebase
    Security & NetworkingWireGuard VPN, Wireshark, Nmap, Metasploit
    IDEs & ToolingCLion, PyCharm, IntelliJ IDEA, VS Code, Git, Selenium (Python, Java)

    Zion: Comprehensive, Adaptive, and Lightweight Hardware Prefetcher

    Research Published

    Technologies: C++, ChampSim, Memory Architecture, Hardware Design

    Designed and implemented Zion - a novel L2 hardware prefetcher built around Independent Temporal-Spatial Modules (ITSM). Segmented delta and stride predictors eliminate inter-stream conflicts that degrade unified-table prefetchers under mixed workloads. Runtime feedback mechanisms - confidence-based filtering, periodic accuracy monitoring, and MSHR-aware control - dynamically regulate prefetch aggressiveness and insertion behavior under memory pressure. Hardware cost: ~9KB storage at L2 cache level.

    Published: Design, Automation and Test in Europe (DATE 2026)

    ChampSim Memory and Performance Optimization

    Research Systems Programming

    Technologies: C++, Multi-threading, Memory Architecture, XZ Compression

    Redesigned core simulation code architecture of ChampSim. While maintaining program equivalency, reduced simulation time up to 90% in multi-core configurations and increased simulation concurrency by 30-40%.

    Utilized Techniques: Algorithm redesign (improved complexities), Data layout optimization (better cache locality), Object orchestration (reduced memory operations), XZ overhead reduction (decreased OS context-switching).

    KV Cache Eviction Policy for Large Language Models

    Research

    Technologies: Python, vLLM Framework, POS Tagging, Memory Optimization

    Engineered a novel approach to KV cache eviction policy using POS tagging to determine token relevance, effectively optimizing memory footprint and performance improvement of LLMs. The strategy ensures performance improvement and resource management within a vLLM framework via a custom BlockCombiner class prioritizing essential tokens for model accuracy.

    Network Infrastructure

    Systems Programming

    Technologies: WireGuard VPN, VPS, NAS, Docker, Linux

    Developed and maintained a multi-site VPN infrastructure using WireGuard across geographically distributed VPS nodes, supporting encrypted cross-regional connectivity and remote client onboarding. Deployed NAS for centralized network storage with local and remote access where wide variety of services were hosted.

    Cryptocurrency Lottery Token

    Blockchain

    Technologies: Solidity, Ethereum, Smart Contracts, React, Web3, Moralis, OpenZeppelin, Metamask

    Developed a blockchain-based lottery token using Smart Contract via Solidity on Ethereum, allowing users to gamble via a Metamask-integrated website. The token ensures fair win probabilities correlated with equitable rewards, employing robust functionality through modern blockchain frameworks.

    Battleship Game with Design Patterns

    Software Engineering

    Technologies: Java, Design Patterns, JUnit, Object-Oriented Programming

    Battleship game in Java implementing four design patterns: Decorator Pattern, Factory Pattern, Command Pattern, and State Pattern. Features include game saving mechanics, resume functionality after game closure, and extensible addon architecture. Includes comprehensive JUnit tests for reliability.

    3-Dimensional Connect 4 Game

    Game Development

    Technologies: Unity, C#, 3D Game Development, Custom Scripts

    Designed and developed a 3D Connect 4 game in Unity with a three-axis board integrating functionality using C# to handle all calculations including win/lose condition checks, free-cam script, border limits, and teleportation functionality.

    Password Manager

    Web Development

    Technologies: Django, Python, Web Framework, Authentication, Database Security

    A working password manager using Django web framework. This website allows users to register, login and logout. The web app enables users to add name tags, passwords, and descriptions visible only to the corresponding users, ensuring secure personal data management.

    GoLang Random Mosaic

    Web Development

    Technologies: Go, HTML, CSS, Web Development, Dynamic UI, Concurrency

    A Go-hosted mosaic webpage utilizing Go's concurrency model for request handling alongside HTML and CSS frontend. Generates a 6×6 grid of random shapes, colors, and characters with dynamic re-randomization and responsive sizing. Demonstrates Go's net/http concurrent request serving and template rendering pipeline.

    Tic Tac Toe Game

    Game Development Systems Programming

    Technologies: C++, OOP, Real-time Input, State Management

    Two-player Tic Tac Toe implemented in C++ with object-oriented architecture across dedicated Game and HowTo classes. Features real-time keypress input via getch() with recursive input validation - invalid or occupied moves are rejected without requiring re-prompting. Win detection covers all 8 axes with a full game state machine managing player turns, draw detection, and session reset for replay.
